behavior_summary: U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit handles an object being hit by recording a 2-byte value under info key 0x020C on the hit object, marking the hit as handled so no further default processing occurs, and then destroying the same object.
The handler treats its first parameter as the object reference/ID and uses it both for the set-info operation and for Item::destroy.
The second parameter is present in the function signature but is not used in the observed code.
Overall gameplay effect: when this object receives a hit it records a small state flag/value, suppresses additional hit processing, and removes the object from the world (cleanup/destruction).
notable_control_flow: U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit pushes the object reference and a constant info key (0x020C) plus a 2-byte value, then invokes the engine 'set info' operation to store that value on the object.
Immediately after setting the info the handler issues a 'process exclude' so subsequent/default hit handling is suppressed for this event.
Finally it calls Item::destroy with the same object reference to remove the item from the game; the sequence is 'set-info -> process-exclude -> destroy'.
open_questions: What is the semantic meaning of info key 0x020C (e.g., a 'marked-as-hit' flag, damage type, or owner id) — this is inferred but not explicit.
What is the exact source and semantic meaning of the indirect 2-byte value written into info 0x020C (constant, derived from the object, or caller-supplied)?
Does process-exclude prevent only default engine 'hit' behavior or also block other scripts/handlers attached to this object? (inferred to stop default handling)
Is Item::destroy immediate (synchronous) or deferred by the engine (affects timing of any chained effects)?

main_function.name: U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to a hit on an object by recording a small info value on that object, preventing further hit processing for this event, and destroying the object.
entry_conditions: Event-driven: invoked when this object receives a hit (Event 6 / U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit).
Called with the object reference/ID as the first parameter ([BP+06h]) and an additional word parameter (unused in observed snippet).

function_map.name: U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for hit events — records info on the object, excludes further processing, and destroys the object.

function_map.name: U8P_LARWEBEW::set info (opcode 77)
function_map.role: Engine opcode to store a key/value pair (info) on an object instance.

function_map.name: U8P_LARWEBEW::process exclude (opcode 78)
function_map.role: Engine opcode to mark the current event/object as excluded/handled to prevent further processing.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Remove/destroy the specified item/object from the game world.

external_calls.purpose: Write a 2-byte value into the object's info field identified by key 0x020C (record state about the hit).
Mark the hit event/object as handled so default or further hit processing is suppressed.
Destroy/remove the object that was hit (cleanup after recording info and excluding processing).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LARWEBEW::gotHit handles an object being hit by recording a 2-byte value under info key 0x020C on the hit object, marking the hit as handled so no further default processing occurs, and then destroying the same object.
